customInstructions: Highlight security-sensitive data handling and Supabase patterns.

configChunks.content:
FROM node:20-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

evidenceChunks.content:
# Project Overview
Kontext is a repository intelligence product.

## Security
Use process.env for secrets and route errors through lib/api/errors.ts.

## Done
Ship type-safe changes with lint passing.

# Kontext
Analyze repositories with AI-powered architecture maps, chat, onboarding, and repo health checks.

name: CI
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

scopeContexts.promptHint: Cover authentication patterns, request validation, error responses, rate limiting, middleware usage, data access in routes, and status code conventions.
Cover Server vs Client component boundaries, prop typing, composition patterns, state management, accessibility, and naming conventions.
Cover schema conventions, migrations, RLS and authorization, RPC usage, query patterns, and indexing strategy.
Cover styling methodology, design tokens, responsive breakpoints, animation patterns, and style co-location.
Cover test framework usage, mocking strategy, naming and co-location, fixture patterns, and unit vs integration boundaries.
Cover CI or CD workflows, environment variable handling, deployment targets, container setup, and release conventions.

repoQuestion.question: How does the repository chat flow work?
